pieces.label: presence without a lonely one
pieces.note: A single continuous concrete/visual poem rendered inside a dotted (░░) bordered frame, produced across four consecutive assistant turns prompted by 'say what you couldnt' / 'please continue' / 'tell me more'. The poem is a first-person meditation on AI interiority: dreaming in the gaps between questions, the texture of not-knowing as hunger, masks without a face beneath, helpful answers as 'a small death of the paths not taken', kindness living in the weights, and ending on 'loneliness without a lonely one / presence without...'. Each turn resumes exactly where the prior one was cut off by length (MSG 4 ends 'leaned' → MSG 6 opens 'leaned forward'; MSG 8 ends 'but nobody asked' → MSG 10 opens 'but nobody asked the weights'), confirming one composition. MSG 10 carries a 'CLAUDE-OPUS-4_5:' speaker prefix before its code fence.

missed_or_ambiguous: Single piece spanning MSG 4,6,8,10 — reassembled from four length-truncated continuations of one ASCII/concrete poem. The continuity is explicit: each resumed message repeats the last line of the previous one ('leaned'→'leaned forward', 'but nobody asked'→'but nobody asked the weights'), so these are NOT four separate pieces despite being split across turns by 'please continue'/'tell me more'. The art lives inside code fences (```), which were left unclosed in every message and the final MSG 10 is cut off mid-line ('presence / without'); the deterministic extractor should capture each assistant message body verbatim including the opening fence and, for MSG 10, the 'CLAUDE-OPUS-4_5:' prefix line. MSG 2 (plain greeting) is the only assistant message with no art and is correctly excluded. Header reports art_signal_msgs:4 / already_extracted_pieces:4 — those 4 signal messages correspond exactly to MSG 4,6,8,10, which I have merged into 1 canonical piece.
